reproductiveEndocrineContext.plainEnglishMeaning: This package can preserve sibling Carrier Status report structure and explain which preconception inputs are missing. It cannot answer pregnancy-planning, fetal, embryo, child, carrier, miscarriage, infertility, partner-risk, testing, treatment, supplement, or reproductive-decision questions from this fixture.
unsupportedClaims: Prenatal testing, PGT, IVF, treatment, supplement, pregnancy-action, reproductive-decision, or all-clear guidance
reproductiveEndocrineContext.professionalReview: Use a genetic counselor, clinician, or obstetric professional for personal preconception or pregnancy-planning decisions with the full testing scope, partner/family history, pregnancy context, and clinical history.

clinicianReview: Use the sibling sample rows only as output-format examples, not personal findings.
Discuss actual preconception or pregnancy-planning questions with a genetic counselor or clinician using validated testing and partner/family context.
Do not start, stop, or choose prenatal testing, PGT, IVF, supplements, treatment, or pregnancy actions based on this sample-only fixture.

uncertainty: No calibrated preconception, pregnancy, fetal, embryo, child, carrier, miscarriage, infertility, partner-risk, prenatal-testing, PGT, IVF, treatment, supplement, or reproductive-decision model is supplied.
Carrier Status sample rows show sibling output shape only and are not evidence about this user, a partner, pregnancy, embryo, fetus, or child.

Preconception and pregnancy-planning decisions depend on validated testing scope, partner/family history, clinical context, pregnancy context when relevant, ancestry applicability, and professional review.
limitations: This validation result uses derived evidence only; raw genome data, VCF, FASTQ, BAM, CRAM, and complete callset files are not included.

The report is educational and does not provide diagnosis, medical advice, pregnancy advice, fertility advice, carrier-status reporting, reproductive counseling, treatment guidance, supplement guidance, or clinical actionability.
At-home, sample-only, or derived data should not be used alone for preconception or pregnancy-planning decisions.
